# Phase 10.0.19 Repository Integrity Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, autonomous repository mutation systems, automated commit-authority systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve repository integrity, architectural traceability, governance/runtime separation, deterministic history, rollback survivability, and long-horizon source-control auditability after legacy stewardship doctrine.

## Repository Hygiene Posture

Current implementation posture: UNSTABLE.

Reason: unrelated runtime/application modifications and many untracked files exist, but no staged changes were detected during pre-build inspection. Phase 10.0.19 must remain path-isolated to governance documentation and planning files.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional repository-integrity doctrine.
- Governance-aware source-control philosophy.
- Architectural traceability survivability framework.
- Repository contamination-prevention doctrine.
- Deterministic commit-history standards.
- Governance/runtime boundary-preservation architecture.
- Source-control survivability philosophy.
- Constitutional rollback survivability standards.
- Architecture-aware repository auditability doctrine.
- Governance traceability continuity framework.
- Phase 10.0.19 repository integrity summary.

## Validation Checklist

- Git status inspected.
- Staged changes inspected.
- Branch divergence inspected.
- Unrelated application/runtime changes reported.
- Mixed-scope worktree state classified.
- Governance/runtime boundary integrity preserved.
- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No autonomous repository mutation system exists.
- No automated commit-authority system exists.

## Review Notes

- Repository integrity is constitutional integrity.
- Source control is governance memory.
- Dirty worktree ambiguity must be classified and reported.
- Staging is a governance act and must remain path-specific.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, autonomous repository mutation systems, automated commit-authority systems, or execution systems.
